findings.title: Watcher callback in start() has 4 levels of nesting
findings.description: The start() method reaches 4 levels of nesting: for-loop > try > subscribe callback > for-loop over events > if extension check > try/catch around onChange. This makes the control flow difficult to follow at a glance. Extracting the watcher callback into a named private method would flatten the nesting in start() and give the callback a descriptive name.

findings.title: Duplicated type assertion for PDF text item extraction
findings.description: The expression casts `item` to `Record<string, unknown>` twice in succession -- once for the type guard and once for the value access. After the `'str' in item` narrowing, TypeScript knows item has a `str` property. The double cast plus verbose conditional can be simplified with a single cast or a helper type. The `typeof ... === 'string'` check after `'str' in item` is also somewhat redundant since pdfjs-dist TextItem.str is always a string.

findings.title: Variable 'textParts' used for two different semantics
findings.description: Both extractPdf and extractXlsx use the variable name 'textParts' but with different semantic granularity (pages vs. sheets). While both are private methods so confusion risk is low, naming them 'pageTexts' and 'sheetTexts' respectively would make the code slightly more self-documenting.

positive_observations: text-chunker.ts is an exemplary pure function: no side effects, named constants for defaults, early guard clauses, only 56 lines, cyclomatic complexity ~4
All source files stay well under the 200-line threshold with clear single-responsibility
FolderMonitor and DocumentProcessor are properly documented as Imperative Shell with class-based design, consistent with project architecture conventions
Extension normalization in FolderMonitor constructor handles both '.txt' and 'txt' input gracefully
Test files use real file I/O with temp directories rather than mocks, following the project's testing philosophy
The integration test demonstrates a complete end-to-end pipeline (extract -> chunk -> embed -> store -> search) with real LanceDB
